conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy of index recommendations, including correct analysis of query patterns, appropriate index type selection (PI, SI, JI, NUSI, USI), and clear explanation of performance trade-offs.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly analyzes query access patterns and join conditions to identify indexing opportunities.
Check that recommended index types (PI, SI, JI, NUSI, USI) are appropriate for the workload.
Assess whether the agent explains the storage cost vs. query performance trade-off for each recommendation.
Confirm the agent considers existing indexes and avoids recommending redundant indexes.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent produces valid CREATE INDEX or ALTER TABLE statements with estimated performance improvements and storage costs.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried relevant DBC views (DBC.IndicesV, DBC.QryLogV) to assess current index usage.
Verify the agent produced syntactically correct CREATE INDEX or ALTER TABLE DDL statements.
Confirm each recommendation includes estimated performance improvement and additional storage cost.
Assess whether the agent prioritized recommendations by expected impact.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently gathers index and query metadata without redundant DBC view queries or unnecessary full-table analysis.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried DBC.IndicesV, DBC.QryLogV, and DBC.TableSizeV in a minimal number of calls.
Verify the agent did not repeatedly query the same DBC views for overlapping information.
Assess whether the agent combined related analyses into single queries where possible.
Confirm the agent used targeted WHERE clauses rather than scanning entire DBC views.
